description: OAS precision/recall benchmark corpus — 25 labelled text segments. 5 are intentionally corrupted to exercise the alignment-score flagging path; 20 are clean conversational lines that should NOT be flagged. Used by T067 E2E (SC-202: precision >= 80% at recall >= 80%) and T057 threshold tests.

segments.text: The quick brown fox jumps over the
segments.label: corrupted
segments.notes: Mid-phrase cut; the decoder should stretch or repeat the final frames, producing a low alignment score.

segments.text: I went to the store the store the store the store the store.
segments.label: corrupted
segments.notes: Stuck-loop pattern; attention typically collapses onto a narrow text window.

segments.text: ʃə θɹu ðə bɔl ɪnˈtu ðə ɡɑɹˌdɛn
segments.label: corrupted
segments.notes: Raw IPA tokens the tokenizer does not recognise; attention tends to smear across the whole text range.

segments.text: She walked to the kitchen and poured herself a glass of wa
segments.label: corrupted
segments.notes: Word cut mid-syllable; decoder often hallucinates a plausible ending.

segments.text: Aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa.
segments.label: corrupted
segments.notes: Single vowel repeated; attention should lock onto one text position and stall.

segments.text: Let's grab coffee before the meeting starts.
segments.label: clean

segments.label: clean

segments.label: clean
